description: The eukaryotic cell cycle in budding yeast is controlled by cyclin-dependent kinases (CDKs), primarily Cdc28 (CDK1), which partners with different cyclins at different cell cycle stages. This elegant system of checkpoints, positive and negative regulators ensures proper progression through G1, S, G2, and M phases, with critical controls at Start, DNA replication, and mitosis.

name: Cell Cycle Control and CDK Regulation
notes: This model represents the core cell cycle control system. Contains 3 OR logic gates (Start checkpoint, DNA replication checkpoint, spindle assembly checkpoint), each with binary yes/no branching. The Start checkpoint evaluates size AND nutrients internally but is represented as a single decision diamond. The yeast model system led to fundamental discoveries applicable to all eukaryotes (2001 Nobel Prize). All 30 nodes are uniquely identified and colored according to the 7-color Programming Framework.
scientificAccuracy: This flowchart is based on Nobel Prize-winning research and verified experimental data from peer-reviewed literature. All molecular mechanisms including cyclin-CDK regulation, checkpoint controls, and APC/C function have been validated against primary research sources. The model represents fundamental principles of eukaryotic cell cycle control discovered in budding yeast and conserved across all eukaryotes (2001 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine).
